description_long: This object is a hand tool with a long, cylindrical wooden handle and a double-sided, pointed metal head. The handle is light brown with visible wood grain texture, and it is smoothly finished, suggesting comfortable grip. The head is made of gray metal, featuring two elongated, tapered blades extending horizontally in opposite directions, each ending in a sharp point. The metal head is securely mounted atop the handle, with a central hub that appears robust and slightly rounded. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or visible branding. The tool is well-suited for heavy-duty digging or breaking hard surfaces in urban environments such as construction sites, parks, or roadside maintenance.
description: A sturdy hand tool with a wooden handle and a double-pointed gray metal head, designed for digging or breaking hard surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the double-pointed metal head and the cylindrical wooden handle beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the metal head and the round handle, with the pointed ends of the head visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the full span of the metal head and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the slim edge of the metal head and the handle's length.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth finish and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_type: Double-pointed, flat metal head with elongated, tapered blades extending horizontally from a central hub.
